Definition: Unique identifier for the scan, following the RFC 4122 format

Enum describing the trigger for the scan: `timer`: scan triggered by timer, set by value in config response and moderated by Android scheduler`location`: scan triggered by detected change in device location

Timestamp of the scan, using the device clock, in UTC, following ISO 8601 and the `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss.sssZ` format Note: Customers may have their extracts (but not real-time feeds) configured to provide this field in the timezone of their choice, in which case it will be in the `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss.sss±hh:mm` format.

Timestamp of Ookla's receipt of the scan, in UTC, following ISO 8601 and the `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss.sssZ` format Note: Customers may have their extracts (but not real-time feeds) configured to provide this field in the timezone of their choice, in which case it will be in the `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss.sss±hh:mm` format.
Timestamp of Ookla's receipt of the scan, in the device's local timezone, following ISO 8601 and the `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss.sss±hh:mm` format
The name of the deivce's local timezone at the time of the scan, using the Olson database timezone name format of `Area/Location`
An identifier for the Android device, durable only up to reinstallation of the app that embeds the Ookla Android SDK, using a 16-character hexadecimal number
Whether the device identifier is valid
The common/marketing name for the device model, based on an Ookla-maintained dataset
The common/marketing name of the device manufacturer, based on an Ookla-maintained dataset
The model of the device as provided by Android APIs
The manufacturer of the device as provided by Android APIs
The brand of the device as provided by Android APIs
The name of the deivce chipset, based on an Ookla-maintained dataset
The manufacturer of the device chipset, based on an Ookla-maintained dataset
The version of the device radio firmware
The version of the Android operating system
The Android API level corresponding to the Android operating system version
The firmware build number of the Android operating system
The Android package name of the app embedding the Ookla SDK, using reverse domain name notation
The version of the Android app embedding the Ookla SDK, as (would be) provided to the Google Play Store
The version of the Ookla SDK embedded in the Android app
The name of the config used by the scan, as set in the Ookla customer portal
The type of the config used by the scan, typically `signal-scan`
An arbitrary JSON object provided by the app to provide additional customer, user, or device context for the scan
Whether the app was in the foreground when the scan was taken

A numeric enum indicating the data connection type: `0`: unknown`1`: cell`2`: wifi`3`: GPRS (2G/3G: General Packet Radio Service)`4`: EDGE (2G: Enhanced Data GSM Evolution)`5`: UMTS (3G: Universal Mobile Telecommunications Service)`6`: CDMA (2G/3G: Code-Division Multiple Access)`7`: EV-DO Rel 0 (3G: Evolution-Data Optimized Release 0)`8`: EV-DO Rev A (3G: Evolution-Data Optimized Revision A)`9`: 1xRTT / CDMA2000 (3G: Single-Carrier Radio Transmission Technology)`10`: HSDPA (3.5G: High-Speed Downlink Packet Access)`11`: HSPA (3.5G: High-Speed Packet Access)`12`: iDEN (2.5G: Integrated Digital Enhanced Network)`13`: eHRPD (3G/4G: Enhanced High-Rate Packet Data)`14`: EV-DO Rev B (3G: Evolution-Data Optimized Revision B)`15`: LTE (4G: Long Term Evolution)`16`: HSUPA (3.5G: High-Speed Uplink Uplink Packet Access)`17`: HSPA+ / HSPAP (3.5G: High-Speed Packet Access Plus)`18`: GSM (2G: Global System for Mobile Communications)`19`: TD-SCDMA (3G: Time-Division Synchronous Code-Division Multiple Access)`20`: IWLAN (Interworking Wireless LAN)`21`: LTE CA (4G: Long Term Evolution Advanced Carrier Aggregation)`22`: ethernet`23`: Bluetooth`24`: NR (5G: New Radio)

A numeric enum indicating whether and what type of network override occured: `0`: no override`1`: LTE with carrier aggregation`2`: LTE advanced pro`3`: NR NSA (non-standalone)`4`: NR NSA (non-standalone) mmWave (millimeter wave)`5`: NR advanced (NR network with data rate that is higher than the generic 5G rates of mmWave, unique bands, or carrier aggregation)
The value of the cellular connection generation or technology family, derived from connected cell info, such as `2G`, `3G`, `4G`, `LTE`, `5G`
Whether the device was in airplane mode when the scan was taken
Whether the device is roaming, specifically, whether the SIM carrier matches the network carrier
Whether the device is roaming internationally, specifically, whether the country of the SIM carrier matches the country where the device is located (eg, based on GPS)
A count of the number of registered cell sites to which the device is connected
A count of the number of unregistered cell sites to which the device is connected

The state of the SIM card: `0`: unknown state; possibly in transition between states`1`: no SIM card is available in the device`2`: locked; requires the user's PIN to unlock`3`: locked; requires the user's PUK to unlock`4`: locked; requires a network PIN to unlock`5`: ready`6`: SIM card is not ready`7`: SIM card error; permanently disabled`8`: SIM card error; present, but faulty`9`: SIM card restricted; present, but not usable due to carrier restrictions

The service state of the device: `0`: in service; normal operation condition; the phone is registered with an operator either in home network or roaming`1`: out of service; phone is not registered with any operator; possibly searching for an operator, not searching, registration denied, or no available radio signal`2`: phone is registered and locked; only emergency numbers are allowed`3`: radio of telephony is explicitly powered off
The name of the SIM card provider, which, in the case of MVNOs, is the name of the carrier that owns the PLMN, based on an Ookla-maintained dataset
The name of the SIM card provider as reported directly by the device, which may be the MVNO, rather than the carrier that owns the PLMN
The Mobile Country Code of the SIM card operator, following the ITU-T E.212 format
The Mobile Network Code of the SIM card operator, following the ITU-T E.212 format
The name of the mobile network operator that the device is connected to
The Mobile Country Code of the network operator that the device is connected to, following the ITU-T E.212 format
The Mobile Network Code of the network operator that the device is connected to, following the ITU-T E.212 format
The most reliable device latitude (in decimal degrees) based on age and accuracy, as reported by GPS and other location services
The most reliable device longitude (in decimal degrees) based on age and accuracy, as reported by GPS and other location services
The most reliable device elevation (in meters) above the WGS-84 reference ellipsoid, based on age and accuracy, as reported by GPS and other location services
The number of meters within which the longitude and latitude are accurate, with `0` indicating inability to establish accuracy
The number of meters within which the elevation is accurate, with `0` indicating inability to establish accuracy
How stale (in milliseconds) the location data is, calculated as the difference between the device's timestamp for the acquired location and the time of the scan

How location coordinates were established: `1`: on-device Android location services (eg, GPS or Fused)`2`: Ookla-inferred location based on IP address (resulting in `null` values for the location elevation, accuracy, and age fields)
The name of the country where the scan was performed
The name of the region (first-order civil entity within a country, such as state, province, or territory) where the scan was performed
If applicable, the name of the region (second-order civil entity within a country, such as a county or parish) where the scan was performed
The postal code (as what would be used to address mail within the country) where the scan was performed

The name of the most granular place the location coordinates could be resolved to, at the level of granularity specified by `place_type` Note: This will typically be an incorporated city or town political entity (corresponding to a `locality` for the `place_type`), but it may duplicate the value of the `country`, `region`, or `subregion`, if greater granularity is unavailable or inapplicable.

The type of place the location coordinates resolved to for the `place_name` field: `locality`: incorporated city or town political entity`neighborhood`: named neighborhood`sublocality_level_1`: civil entity hierarchically below a locality`sublocality_level_2`: civil entity hierarchically below sublocality_level_1`sublocality_level_3`: civil entity hierarchically below sublocality_level_2`sublocality_level_4`: civil entity hierarchically below sublocality_level_3`sublocality_level_5`: civil entity hierarchically below sublocality_level_4`administrative_area_level_1`: first-order civil entity hierarchically below a country ("region"), such as states in the United States`administrative_area_level_2`: second-order civil entity hierarchically below a country ("subregion"), such as counties in the United States`administrative_area_level_3`: third-order civil entity hierarchically below a country`administrative_area_level_4`: fourth-order civil entity hierarchically below a country`administrative_area_level_5`: fifth-order civil entity hierarchically below a country`colloquial_area`: commonly used alternative name fo the place`country`: national political entity`postal town`: grouping of geographic areas, such as locality and sublocality, used for mailing addresses in some countries
Whether the coordinates reported by the device represent a valid location
Whether the user granted the app fine location permission
Whether the user granted the app coarse location permission
Whether the user granted the app background location permission
Whether the user granted the app phone state permission (eg, phone number, cellular network information, status of calls)
Whether the app secured internet access permission from the operating system
Whether the app secured network state permission (for monitoring network connections in general) from the operating system
Whether the app secured wifi state permission (for monitoring wifi connectivity) from the operating system
Whether the app secured boot completed permission (for restarting jobs after a reboot) from the operating system
Whether the app secured billing permission (for in-app purchases) from the operating system
For GSM technology, the Received Signal Strength Indicator, measured in dBm in the range [-113, -51]
For WCDMA technology, the ratio of the energy of pilot signal per chip (Ec) to the noise density (No), measured in dB in the range [-24, 1]
For GSM and WCDMA technology, the 16-bit integer representing a cell's Location Area Code within the operator's system
For WCDMA and UMTS technology, the Primary Scrambling Code in the range [0, 512]

For LTE technology, the 16-bit integer representing the Tracking Area Code that facilitates handoff between cells, and which can be prepended by the operator MCC and MNC to generate the Tracking Area Identity
For LTE technology, the Physical Cell Identifier in the range [0, 503] that identifies the physical LTE cell the device is connected to

The identifier of the cell site the device is connected to, represented by an integer within one of the following ranges, based on technology: GSM (2G): [0, 65535], representing the Base Tranceiver Station Identifier (BTS-ID)UMTS (3G): [0, 268435455], representing the LCIDLTE (4G): [0, 268435455], representing the e-UTRAN Cell Identifier (ECI)
For LTE technology, the 28-bit integer representing the Evolved (E-UTRAN) Node B identifier of the cell site the device is connected to
For 3G technology, the 12-bit integer representing the Radio Network Controller Identifier of the cell site that the device is connected to

For GSM technology, the Absolute Radio-Frequency Channel Number, used to calculate the exact frequency of the radio channel, represented by an integer within one of the following ranges: GSM900: [1, 124]GSM1800: [125, 885]
For UMTS technology, the 16-bit integer representing the UTRA Absolute Radio-Frequency Channel Number that identifies the channel within the frequency bands used by UMTS UTRA

For LTE technology, the integer in the range [0, 70645] representing the E-UTRA Absiolute Radio-Frequency Channel Number that identifies the LTE band and carrier frequency for uplink and downlink and which is independent of channel bandwidth
For LTE technology, the Reference Signal Received Power, measured in dBm in the range [-144, -44]
For LTE technology, the Reference Signal Received Quality, measured in dB in the range [-20, -3]
For LTE technology, the Received Signal Strength Indicator, measured in dBm in the range [-113, -51]
For LTE technology, the Reference Signal Signal-to-Noise Ratio, measured in dB
For LTE technology, the Timing Advance, measured in Ts (1 / 30720000 seconds)
For LTE technology, the Channel Quality Indicator, represented by a code in the range [1, 15] that maps to a modulation scheme
The cell bandwidth (in kHz) for the primary serving cell.
A JSON array of integers representing the cell bandwidths (in kHz) of all serving cells
Whether the device is using carrier aggregation
The wifi state at the time of the scan: `0`: disabling`1`: disabled`2`: enabling`3`: enabled`4`: unknown
The wifi Received Signal Strength Indicator, measured in dBm
The frequency (in MHz) of the wifi connection to the router
The channel number of the wifi connection to the number (corresponding to the frequency)
The width of the wifi channel (in MHz), where dual 80-MHz channels are reported as a single `160` value

For NR technology, the Synchronization Signal Reference Signal Received Power (the linear average over the power contributions of the resource elements that carry secondary synchronization signals), measured in Watts

For NR technology, the Synchronization Signal Reference Signal Received Quality (the ratio of N×SS-RSRP and NR carrier RSSI, where N is the number of resource block in the NR carrier RSSI measurement bandwidth)

For NR technology, the Synchronization Signal Signal-to-noise and Interference Ratio (the ratio of the linear average over the power contribution of the resource elements carrying secondary synchronization signals to the linear average of the noise and interference power contribution over the resource elements within the same frequency bandwidth)

Nor NR technology, the Channel State Information Reference Signal Received Power (the linear average over the power contributions of the resource elements that carry CSI reference signals configured for RSRP measurements within the considered measurement frequency bandwidth in the configured CSI reference signal occasions), measured in Watts
For NR technology, the Channel State Information Reference Signal Received Quality (the ratio of N×CSI-RSRP to CSI-RSSI, where N is the number of resource blocks in the CSI-RSSI measurement bandwith)

For NR technology, the Channel State Information Signal-to-noise and Interference Radio (the ratio of the linear average over the power contribution of the resource elements carrying CSI reference signals to the linear average of the noise and interference power contribution over the resource element carrying CSI reference signals within the same frequency bandwidth)
For NR technology, a computed value characterizing the overall signal quality: `0`: none or unknown`1`: poor`2`: moderate`3`: good`4`: great
For NR technology, the Arbitrary Strength Unit, an integer value proportional to the Reference Signal Received Power
For NR technology, the Absolute Radio Frequency Channel Number (a code that specifies a pair of reference frequencies used for transmission and reception)
For NR technology, the NR Cell Identity, a 36-bit unsigned integer, representing the 5G gNB (logical radio node) and its local cell identity
For NR technology, the Physical Cell ID, an integer in the range [0, 1007], representing individual cells on the radio side

For NR technology, the Tracking Area Code, the 16-bit integer representing the Tracking Area Code that facilitates handoff between cells, and which can be prepended by the operator MCC and MNC to generate the Tracking Area Identity
For NR technology, the Mobile Country Code of the cell the device is connected to
Nor NR technology, the Movile Network Code of the cell the device is connected to
Whether the the cellular access network technoloy is NR, even while connected to wifi as the active data bearer

For NR technology, the state of the on-device radio: `0`: None; the device isn't camped on an LTE cell or the LTE cell doesn't support E-UTRA-NR Dual Connectivity (EN-DC).`1`: Restricted; the device is camped on an LTE cell that supports E-UTRA-NR Dual Connectivity (EN-DC), but either the use of dual connectivity with NR (DCNR) is restricted, or NR is not supported by the selected PLMN.`2`: Not restricted; the device is camped on an LTE cell that supports E-UTRA-NR Dual Connectivity (EN-DC) and both the use of dual connectivity with NR (DCNR) is not restricted, and NR is supported by the selected PLMN.`3`: Connected; the device is camped on an LTE cell that supports E-UTRA-NR Dual Connectivity (EN-DC) and also connected to at least one 5G cell as a secondary serving cell.

For NR technology, the 5G frequency range in use: `0`: unknown`1`: frequency is below 1 GHz`2`: frequency is between 1 GHz and 3 GHz`3`: frequency is between 3 GHz and 6 GHz`4`: frequency is above 6 GHz (mmWave)
Whether NR technology is supported by the selected PLMN

Whether any of the following fields (formatted in snake_case) were sourced from Android's TelephonyManager, rather than from CellInfoNr: ss_rsrp, ss_rsrq, ss_sinr, csi_rsrp, csi_rsrq, csi_sinr, nr_level, nr_arfcn, nci, nr_pci, nr_tac, nr_mcc, nr_mnc
A JSON array of integers representing the 5G bands in use by the device
The battery level percentage at the time of the scan
The maximum possible battery level percentage at the time of the scan; likely to always be `100`
Whether and what kind of power source the device is connected to: `0`: not connected to a power source`1`: AC`2`: dock`4`: USB`8`: wireless charger
Whether a battery is installed in the device
Status of the battery: `1`: unknown`2`: charging`3`: discharging`4`: not charging`5`: full
The technology of the device battery
The battery temperature (in tenths of a degree Centigrade) at the time of the scan
The battery voltage (in millivolts) at the time of the scan
Whether the device is in an interactive state, meaning it is awake and ready to be interacted with by the user, usually corresponding to the screen being on
Whether the device is in power save mode, signaling to applications that they should redice their functionality in order to conserve battery

Whether the device is in idle mode (a low-power use state), typically reflecting having been sitting unused and unmoving for a sufficiently long period of time and may involve turning off network access to apps
